title: The AI Boom: How Memory Chips Are Changing the Global Economy
subtitle: Record profits for tech giants highlight the massive demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure.

summary.en: Global demand for artificial intelligence technology is driving record profits for major semiconductor companies. Samsung Electronics recently reported a massive increase in earnings due to the high need for specialized memory chips. Analysts suggest that this trend is fueled by the rapid growth of AI data centers worldwide. While the industry is seeing unprecedented success, experts warn that supply shortages may continue for some time. Countries across Asia are now investing heavily in local manufacturing to keep up with this competitive market.
summary.zh: 全球對人工智慧技術的需求，正推動大型半導體公司獲利創下新高。三星電子近期因對特殊記憶體晶片的需求強勁，獲利大幅成長。分析師認為，此趨勢是由全球人工智慧資料中心的快速擴張所帶動。儘管產業正經歷前所未有的成功，專家仍警告供應短缺的情況可能會持續一段時間。亞洲各國目前正投入大量資金發展在地製造，以跟上這個競爭激烈的市場。

paragraphs.en: The global technology industry is currently experiencing a major shift, driven by the rapid rise of artificial intelligence (AI). As companies around the world race to build more powerful AI systems, the demand for the specialized computer parts that power them—known as semiconductors or chips—has reached record levels. This trend has created a massive financial boost for the companies that manufacture these essential components.
paragraphs.zh: 全球科技產業目前正經歷一場由人工智慧（AI）快速崛起所帶動的重大轉變。隨著世界各地的企業競相打造更強大的人工智慧系統，對於驅動這些系統的特殊電腦零件——即半導體或晶片——的需求已達到歷史新高。這股趨勢為製造這些關鍵組件的企業帶來了巨大的財務成長。

paragraphs.en: According to BBC Business, the South Korean technology giant Samsung Electronics recently announced that it expects its profits to jump by 19 times compared to previous periods. This expected growth is largely due to the global need for AI memory chips. These chips are vital for the data centers that allow AI programs to process information quickly. Samsung’s recent forecast suggests it made nearly 90 trillion won between April and June, marking its third record-breaking quarter in a row. This performance shows just how much the AI boom is changing the financial landscape for major tech manufacturers.
paragraphs.zh: 根據英國廣播公司商業新聞（BBC Business）報導，南韓科技巨頭三星電子（Samsung Electronics）近期宣布，預計其獲利將比以往同期成長十九倍。這波預期成長主要歸功於全球對人工智慧記憶體晶片的需求。這些晶片對於讓人工智慧程式能快速處理資訊的資料中心而言至關重要。三星近期的預測顯示，其在四月至六月間的獲利接近九十兆韓元，創下連續第三季打破紀錄的佳績。此表現顯示人工智慧熱潮正大幅改變大型科技製造商的財務版圖。

paragraphs.en: Industry experts note that this situation is unique. Marc Einstein, an analyst from Counterpoint Research, described the current market as a "tidal wave" of demand. He explained that the supply of these chips is limited, which has allowed companies like Samsung to increase their prices. When demand is higher than the available supply, prices naturally go up. This has created a very profitable environment for the few companies capable of producing the high-quality chips needed for modern AI infrastructure.
paragraphs.zh: 產業專家指出，這種情況相當罕見。市場研究機構 Counterpoint Research 的分析師馬克·愛因斯坦（Marc Einstein）將當前市場形容為需求「如潮水般湧現」。他解釋說，這些晶片的供應有限，這讓三星等公司得以調漲價格。當需求大於現有供給時，價格自然會上漲。這為少數有能力生產現代人工智慧基礎設施所需之高品質晶片的企業，創造了極為有利可圖的環境。

paragraphs.en: However, this focus on AI has consequences for other parts of the technology world. Research firm IDC reported that the massive need for chips in data centers is different from anything the memory industry has seen before. Because so much manufacturing capacity is being used to create AI-specific chips, there is less supply available for everyday electronics, such as personal computers and smartphones. Bryan Ma, a researcher at IDC, warned that supplies will likely remain tight through next year because the demand from AI data centers shows no signs of slowing down.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，這種對人工智慧的聚焦也對科技界的其他領域產生了影響。研究公司國際數據資訊（IDC）指出，資料中心對晶片的龐大需求，與記憶體產業過去所見的情況截然不同。由於大量的製造產能被用於生產人工智慧專用晶片，導致個人電腦與智慧型手機等日常電子產品的可用供應量減少。國際數據資訊的研究員布萊恩·馬（Bryan Ma）警告，由於人工智慧資料中心的需求絲毫沒有放緩跡象，供應短缺的情況很可能會持續到明年。

paragraphs.en: This trend is not limited to Samsung. Other major players in the industry, such as Nvidia, have also seen record-breaking sales. Nvidia, which designs chips used by many of the world's largest tech companies, reported revenue topping $80 billion earlier this year. Despite these strong numbers, some investors have expressed caution. When Nvidia’s stock price fell after its record-breaking report, some analysts suggested that investors are becoming worried about rising competition in the sector. Even Samsung saw its share price fall by almost 7% on the day of its announcement, as some investors had hoped for even higher profits.
paragraphs.zh: 這股趨勢並非僅限於三星。輝達（Nvidia）等產業內的其他主要參與者也創下了銷售紀錄。輝達為全球許多大型科技公司設計晶片，今年稍早公布的營收突破八百億美元。儘管數據亮眼，部分投資人仍表達了謹慎態度。當輝達在公布破紀錄報告後股價下跌時，一些分析師認為投資人正開始擔憂該領域日益激烈的競爭。甚至三星在發布消息當天，股價也下跌了近百分之七，因為部分投資人原本期待更高的獲利。

paragraphs.en: To stay competitive, countries are now making massive investments in their own manufacturing capabilities. In June, South Korea revealed plans to invest at least $880 billion in projects led by Samsung and SK Hynix. The goal is to build more chip factories and secure the country's position as a leader in the global semiconductor market. Other Asian nations, including Japan, China, and Taiwan, are also spending heavily to build new factories. These countries understand that whoever controls the production of AI chips will have a significant advantage in the future global economy.
paragraphs.zh: 為了保持競爭力，各國目前正對自身的製造能力進行大規模投資。六月時，南韓揭露了投資至少八千八百億美元的計畫，由三星與 SK 海力士（SK Hynix）主導相關專案。其目標是興建更多晶片工廠，並確保該國在全球半導體市場的領先地位。包括日本、中國與台灣在內的其他亞洲國家，也正投入鉅資興建新廠。這些國家深知，誰能掌控人工智慧晶片的生產，誰就能在未來的全球經濟中占據顯著優勢。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the relationship between AI development and chip manufacturing will remain a key focus for economists and investors. While the current "AI boom" has brought record profits to companies like Samsung and Nvidia, it has also created a fragile supply chain. As long as the world continues to build more AI data centers, the pressure on the semiconductor industry will remain high. Whether these companies can continue to meet this demand without causing further shortages for other electronics remains a major question. For now, the global tech sector is moving forward at a rapid pace, with memory chips serving as the foundation for the next generation of digital technology.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，人工智慧發展與晶片製造之間的關係，仍將是經濟學家與投資人關注的焦點。雖然當前的「人工智慧熱潮」為三星與輝達等公司帶來了創紀錄的獲利，但也造成了脆弱的供應鏈。只要全球持續興建更多人工智慧資料中心，半導體產業所承受的壓力就將維持在高檔。這些企業能否在不導致其他電子產品進一步短缺的情況下滿足此需求，仍是一個重大疑問。目前，全球科技產業正以飛快的速度向前邁進，而記憶體晶片正作為下一代數位技術的基石。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Difficult to obtain because of limited supply.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（緊的），這裡形容供應不足。文中：Bryan Ma, a researcher at IDC, warned that supplies will likely remain tight through next year because the demand from AI data centers shows no signs of slowing down.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Exceeding or reaching more than a specific amount.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（頂部），這裡作動詞用，表示金額超過。文中：Nvidia, which designs chips used by many of the world's largest tech companies, reported revenue topping $80 billion earlier this year.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Happening one after another without interruption.
keyTerms.noteZh: 容易誤以為與「排隊」有關，實際指連續發生。文中：Samsung’s recent forecast suggests it made nearly 90 trillion won between April and June, marking its third record-breaking quarter in a row.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Samsung profits jump 1,800% as AI chip sales soar

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the primary reason for the recent financial success of Samsung Electronics?

options.A: A decrease in the production costs of everyday electronics.
options.B: The global demand for specialized memory chips used in AI data centers.
options.C: A significant increase in the sales of personal computers and smartphones.
options.D: Government subsidies provided to South Korean tech companies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，三星電子利潤預期大幅成長，主要是因為全球對於 AI 記憶體晶片的迫切需求，這些晶片對於 AI 資料中心處理資訊至關重要。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到 AI 需求反而導致日常電子產品晶片供應吃緊；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章指出日常電子產品的供應反而因為產能被 AI 晶片佔用而減少；選項 D 雖然提到政府投資，但那是為了未來競爭力，而非三星近期利潤暴增的主因。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the impact of the AI boom on the broader consumer electronics market?

options.A: It has made personal computers and smartphones more affordable for the average user.
options.B: It has encouraged manufacturers to produce more high-quality chips for everyday devices.
options.C: It has created a supply shortage for devices that do not use AI-specific chips.
options.D: It has led to a decrease in the overall number of data centers being built.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，由於製造能力大量集中於 AI 專用晶片，導致一般電子產品（如電腦、手機）的晶片供應減少，且供應短缺的情況預計會持續到明年。選項 A 與事實相反，供應短缺通常會導致價格上漲而非更便宜；選項 B 錯誤，因為產能被 AI 晶片排擠，廠商無暇多生產日常裝置晶片；選項 D 錯誤，文章明確指出 AI 資料中心的需求沒有減緩的跡象。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what does the author mean by describing the current market demand as a 'tidal wave'?

options.A: A sudden, overwhelming, and powerful increase in demand.
options.B: A cyclical pattern that repeats every few years.
options.C: A dangerous trend that will soon destroy the tech industry.
options.D: A quiet and steady growth that is easy to manage.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在第三段中，分析師 Marc Einstein 用「tidal wave」（海嘯）來形容市場需求，意指需求量極大且來勢洶洶，難以抵擋。選項 B 錯誤，海嘯並非週期性現象；選項 C 雖然提到危險，但文章強調的是利潤空間，而非毀滅；選項 D 與「海嘯」帶來的強烈、不可抗力的意象完全相反。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the global technology industry?

options.A: The stock market is the only reliable way to measure the success of AI companies.
options.B: Nvidia is the sole leader in the global semiconductor market, surpassing all other competitors.
options.C: AI development is driving a semiconductor boom that brings both massive profits and supply chain instability.
options.D: Countries are failing to invest enough in their local chip manufacturing capabilities.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文的核心在於探討 AI 興起如何重塑半導體產業，一方面為三星、Nvidia 等大廠帶來空前的獲利（第二、五段），另一方面也造成了供應鏈的脆弱與日常電子產品的短缺（第四、七段），這是一個雙面刃的現象。選項 A 太過狹隘；選項 B 忽略了三星等其他大廠的角色；選項 D 與第六段內容矛盾，因為各國正投入巨資以提升製造能力。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
